html:
Johns Manville Membrane Systems on Laredo Roofs

Johns Manville is one of the oldest manufacturers in the roofing industry, with a corporate history stretching back to the 1800s, and it operates today as a subsidiary of Berkshire Hathaway. That combination of long institutional history and a financially stable parent company is part of why their product lines show up regularly across our proposals, particularly on jobs where modified bitumen or built-up roofing is the right system alongside their single-ply offerings.

A Company That Predates Modern Roofing Chemistry

Johns Manville's roots go back to insulation and building materials manufacturing well before single-ply membranes existed as a product category, which means the company's built-up and modified bitumen roofing lines carry a manufacturing history that predates most of the current single-ply market. That history matters less for a warehouse roof going up today than for the older downtown Laredo buildings where a modified bitumen re-roof is the right call, where a manufacturer with decades of specific experience in that system carries real weight.

TPO, PVC, and the BUR/Modified Bitumen Side

Johns Manville's current catalog spans TPO and PVC single-ply membranes for standard warehouse and retail work, plus a substantial built-up and SBS modified bitumen line for buildings suited to those systems. Having a manufacturer that produces both categories under one roof lets us keep sourcing consistent on a project that mixes system types across additions or phased construction, which happens more often than owners expect on buildings that have been expanded piecemeal over the years.

Insulation Manufacturing Alongside Roofing

Johns Manville's broader business includes a substantial insulation manufacturing line separate from roofing membrane, which is a natural extension of the company's original building-materials focus. On a roof project that also involves an insulation upgrade - which comes up often on the older modified bitumen re-roofs described on our SBS page - sourcing compatible insulation and membrane from the same manufacturer simplifies the documentation and avoids questions about component compatibility that a mixed-manufacturer assembly can raise.

Berkshire Hathaway Ownership and Stability

Johns Manville has operated as a Berkshire Hathaway subsidiary for over two decades, which gives the company a financial stability that matters when an owner is relying on a multi-decade warranty term. A manufacturer's ability to still be in business and honoring warranty obligations fifteen or twenty years after installation is a real, practical consideration, beyond any brand-reputation talking point.

  • System type - single-ply, modified bitumen, or built-up - matched to the specific building and deck
  • Product specifications pulled current for the exact system being installed
  • Fire-watch and application method planned separately for any torch-applied component
  • Warranty terms reviewed in full with the owner before contract signing
  • Documentation filed to the manufacturer's specific registration requirements

Reading the Warranty Fine Print

As with every manufacturer we work with, Johns Manville's warranty terms vary by product line, installation method, and how the project is documented at completion. We don't summarize warranty coverage in general terms in a proposal; we pull the specific document for the product being installed and walk the owner through what's actually covered, what voids coverage, and what ongoing maintenance the warranty assumes.

Where We See Johns Manville Specified Most Often Locally

On our Laredo project list, Johns Manville tends to come up most on the older downtown re-roofs where modified bitumen is the right system, and on mixed-portfolio jobs where an owner wants one manufacturer relationship covering both a modified bitumen building and a newer TPO warehouse. It's less often our first call on a pure new-construction cross-dock warehouse, where several other manufacturers compete just as closely on price and lead time.

When we put together a proposal that includes multiple manufacturer options, Johns Manville's built-up and modified bitumen catalog is usually the deciding factor over a manufacturer like Carlisle or Versico that leans more heavily toward single-ply, not the TPO or PVC side of their business where the competition is closer to even. We tell owners plainly which part of a manufacturer's catalog is genuinely differentiated versus which part is comparable to what several other manufacturers also offer.

Insulation manufacturing is also a substantial part of Johns Manville's broader building-materials business, separate from their roofing membrane and modified bitumen lines, which is worth knowing on a project where insulation and membrane are both being specified from the same source.

Questions About Johns Manville Roofing Systems

How old is Johns Manville as a company?

The company's history in building materials manufacturing dates back to the 1800s, making it one of the older manufacturers currently active in commercial roofing.

Is Johns Manville only a modified bitumen manufacturer?

No, their current catalog includes TPO and PVC single-ply membranes alongside built-up and SBS modified bitumen systems.

Does Berkshire Hathaway ownership affect product quality?

We can't speak to internal manufacturing decisions, but the financial stability of a Berkshire Hathaway subsidiary is a relevant factor for owners relying on a long-term warranty from the company.

Would you use Johns Manville for a torch-applied modified bitumen roof downtown?

It's one of the manufacturers we'd consider for that application, evaluated against the specific building alongside other manufacturers with comparable product lines.

What should I ask about before choosing a Johns Manville warranty?

Ask specifically what voids the coverage, what maintenance and inspection obligations it assumes, and whether the warranty covers material only or material and labor - we go through all of that before a contract is signed.
